nodes.name: file:ml/eval_robustness.py
observations: Python script (142 lines) that stress-tests the trained Transformer ONNX model under realistic noise/perturbation conditions
Evaluates 6 robustness slices: clean, landmark_jitter, dropped_frames, missing_z, rotated, scale_shift
Writes dynamic_robustness_report.md with accuracy degradation analysis
Imports onnxruntime, sklearn.metrics, synth_sequences, seq_features
NFR-9/NFR-10 release gate analysis for the PDR

nodes.name: function:ml/eval_robustness.py:evaluate
observations: Runs a single robustness slice through normalize_batch, ONNX inference, argmax, and accuracy_score
Parameters: X_raw_slice (np.ndarray), name (str)
Returns float accuracy

nodes.name: function:ml/eval_robustness.py:rotate_clip
observations: Rotates a full landmark clip around the first-frame wrist by a given angle in degrees
Parameters: clip, deg
Returns rotated clip as np.ndarray

nodes.name: file:ml/features.py
observations: Python script (38 lines) providing static landmark→feature vector normalization
Pipeline: translate wrist to origin, scale by wrist→middle-MCP distance, flatten to 63 floats
Contract between Python training and JS browser inference
Exports: normalize()

nodes.name: function:ml/features.py:normalize
observations: Normalizes (..., 21, 3) landmarks to (..., 63) feature vectors
Translates by wrist position and scales by wrist-to-middle-MCP distance
Returns flat float32 array

nodes.name: file:ml/models/convert_to_onnx.py
observations: Python script (127 lines) that converts trained models to ONNX format for browser deployment with onnxruntime-web
Likely handles model loading, ONNX export configuration, and output serialization
Part of the model deployment pipeline targeting ml/models/ directory

nodes.name: file:ml/models/train_dynamic_signs.py
observations: Python script (349 lines) that trains the dynamic sign classification model
Likely processes (N, T, 21, 3) landmark sequences for dynamic sign recognition
Probably uses the synth_sequences synthetic dataset and seq_features normalization
Outputs an ONNX model and eval report to ml/models/

nodes.name: file:ml/models/train_fingerspell_mlp.py
observations: Python script (109 lines) that trains a fingerspelling MLP classifier
Likely uses static landmark features from features.py and synth_landmarks synthetic data
Smaller model focused on fingerspelling letter classification

nodes.name: file:ml/data/dataset.py
observations: Python script (230 lines) providing base dataset class and data loading utilities
Likely defines Dataset base class used by all real dataset loaders (WLASL, ChicagoFSWild, MS-ASL, in-house)
Probably handles train/val/test splitting, caching to .npz, and shape contracts (N, 21, 3) for static and (N, T, 21, 3) for dynamic

nodes.name: file:ml/data/processing.py
observations: Python script (456 lines) providing data preprocessing and augmentation utilities
Likely includes landmark normalization, frame interpolation, noise injection, rotation augmentation
Probably shared across all dataset loaders in ml/data/
